---
title: "Abstract example"
author: "Piotr Dyszewski (Uniwersytet Wrocławski)"
github: "https://github.com/pdyszewski/"
homepage: "https://sites.google.com/site/piotrdyszewski/"
categories:
  - example
  - random walk
---

<p>
  <a href="{{< meta github >}}" 
     class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm"
     target="_blank" 
     rel="noopener noreferrer">
    GitHub
  </a>
  <a href="{{< meta homepage >}}" 
     class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm"
     target="_blank" 
     rel="noopener noreferrer">
    Website
  </a>
</p>

This is a sample abstract to showcase the flexibility of the HTML format.
We still support LaTeX commands
\begin{equation*}
	\int_\mathbb{R} f(x) \mathrm{d} x = \lim_{n \to \infty} \sum_{k} f(x_k) \Delta x_k.
\end{equation*}

**Theorem.** Theorems are stated as such, for example
\begin{equation*}
	1+1=2
\end{equation*}

We support images

<figure data-latex-placement="h">
<img src="obrazek.png" style="width:100.0%" />
<figcaption>Figure caption</figcaption>
</figure>

We also support multimedia embedding

<iframe width="100%" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/0_GvG1ch8m0?si=ZOifSAoVS5Vvpq9X&amp;controls=0" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

We also support interactive elements (e.g. JavaScript). Here is a random walk on a random graph

<div id="sketch-rw" style="max-width: 900px; margin: 0 auto;"></div>

<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/p5@1.9.0/lib/p5.min.js"></script>
<script>
(() => {
  const sketch = (p) => {
    // --- parametry ---
    const N = 40;           // liczba węzłów
    const K = 3;            // ile najbliższych sąsiadów łączymy (min. 2-4)
    const STEP_EVERY = 12;   // co ile klatek wykonujemy krok (mniejsze = szybciej)
    const TAIL = 120;       // długość ogona (historia pozycji)
    const PADDING = 40;

    let nodes = [];
    let adj = [];
    let current = 0;
    let tail = [];
    let frameCounter = 0;

    function dist2(a, b) {
      const dx = a.x - b.x, dy = a.y - b.y;
      return dx*dx + dy*dy;
    }

    function buildGraph() {
      nodes = [];
      adj = Array.from({length: N}, () => []);
      tail = [];

      // węzły w losowych pozycjach
      for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        nodes.push({
          x: p.random(PADDING, p.width - PADDING),
          y: p.random(PADDING, p.height - PADDING),
        });
      }

      // k-NN (symetrycznie)
      for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        const dists = [];
        for (let j = 0; j < N; j++) if (j !== i) {
          dists.push({ j, d: dist2(nodes[i], nodes[j]) });
        }
        dists.sort((a,b) => a.d - b.d);
        for (let t = 0; t < K; t++) {
          const j = dists[t].j;
          if (!adj[i].includes(j)) adj[i].push(j);
          if (!adj[j].includes(i)) adj[j].push(i);
        }
      }

      current = Math.floor(p.random(N));
      tail.push(current);
    }

    function stepWalk() {
      const neigh = adj[current];
      if (neigh.length === 0) return; // (nie powinno się zdarzyć przy k-NN)
      current = neigh[Math.floor(p.random(neigh.length))];

      tail.push(current);
      if (tail.length > TAIL) tail.shift();
    }

    p.setup = () => {
      // dopasuj do kontenera
      const el = document.getElementById("sketch-rw");
      const w = Math.min(900, el.clientWidth || 900);
      p.createCanvas(w, 520).parent(el);

      buildGraph();
    };

    p.windowResized = () => {
      const el = document.getElementById("sketch-rw");
      const w = Math.min(900, el.clientWidth || 900);
      p.resizeCanvas(w, 520);
      buildGraph();
    };

    p.draw = () => {
      p.background(250);

      // co STEP_EVERY klatek wykonaj krok
      frameCounter++;
      if (frameCounter % STEP_EVERY === 0) stepWalk();

      // --- rysowanie krawędzi ---
      p.stroke(210);
      p.strokeWeight(1);
      for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        for (const j of adj[i]) if (j > i) {
          p.line(nodes[i].x, nodes[i].y, nodes[j].x, nodes[j].y);
        }
      }

      // --- ogon (ścieżka) ---
      p.noFill();
      p.stroke(30);
      p.strokeWeight(2);
      p.beginShape();
      for (let idx = 0; idx < tail.length; idx++) {
        const v = nodes[tail[idx]];
        p.vertex(v.x, v.y);
      }
      p.endShape();

      // --- węzły ---
      for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        const v = nodes[i];

        // zwykłe węzły
        p.noStroke();
        p.fill(245);
        p.circle(v.x, v.y, 12);

        p.stroke(120);
        p.noFill();
        p.circle(v.x, v.y, 12);
      }

      // --- aktualna pozycja ---
      const c = nodes[current];
      p.noStroke();
      p.fill(255, 80, 80);
      p.circle(c.x, c.y, 18);

      // opis
      p.noStroke();
      p.fill(60);
      p.textSize(14);
      //p.text(`Spacer losowy po grafie (N=${N}, K=${K})  |  aktualny węzeł: ${current}`, 12, 22);
      p.text(`left click: restart  |  Space bar: stop/run`, 12, 42);
    };

    let paused = false;
    p.mousePressed = () => buildGraph();
    p.keyPressed = () => {
      if (p.key === ' ') paused = !paused;
      if (paused) p.noLoop(); else p.loop();
    };
  };

  new p5(sketch);
})();
</script>

List references manually using numbers in square brackets in math
environment: $[1]$, $[2]$, etc.

**Bibliography**

$[1]$ Ken-Iti, S. (1999). *Lévy Processes and Infinitely Divisible
Distributions*. Cambridge University Press.

$[2]$ Jane Smith. \"Title of the Article.\" *Journal Name*, vol. X,
no. Y, Year, pp. Z.

$[3]$ [Wikipedia article about
Wrocław](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wroc%C5%82aw)

$[4]$ [Website of the Faculty of Pure and Applied Mathematics, Wrocław
University of Science and Technology](http://wmat.pwr.edu.pl/)

$[5]$ [Website of the Mathematical Institute, University of
Wrocław](http://www.math.uni.wroc.pl/)
